CREATE TABLE test_values (id INT, value INT);
SELECT * FROM test_values WHERE value > (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_values WHERE id = 2);
SELECT a.id FROM test_values a JOIN test_values b ON a.value > b.value WHERE b.id = 1;
SELECT id, value FROM test_values WHERE value > (SELECT MIN(value) FROM test_values) + 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_values WHERE value > (SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_values);
SELECT id, value FROM test_values WHERE value > (SELECT value FROM test_values WHERE id = 1) AND id != 1;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_values WHERE value > 2) > 0 AS has_greater_than_two;
SELECT a.id FROM test_values a, test_values b WHERE a.value > b.value AND a.id != b.id;
SELECT value FROM test_values ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 1 WHERE value > 1;
